citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The attending veterinarian notified the inspector that an IACUC approved protocol had not been followed by a principle
investigator. The protocol covers training by the Animal Resources Program staff with various species housed at UAB. On
February 9, 2026, two non-naïve rabbits (rabbits that have undergone procedures) were transferred to this protocol and
used for a training lab. At the time of training, the protocol indicated that animals used would be naïve rabbits (rabbits that
would not have undergone procedures prior to use on this protocol). A modification request for the use of non-naïve
animals was under review but had not been approved by the IACUC at the time of the animal transfer. The IACUC
determined that no animal welfare concerns occurred at the time of the training lab. The principle investigator has since
been retrained and the modification to the protocol has now been approved. Failure to follow an approved protocol may
adversely affect the health and well-being of the animals on the protocol. Implementing changes to protocols without
IACUC review and approval prevents the IACUC from providing the necessary oversight of animal activities. The IACUC,
as an agent of the research facility, shall review and approve, require modifications in (to secure approval) or withhold
approval of proposed significant changes regarding the care and use of animals in ongoing activities. Corrected prior to
the time of inspection on 2/27/2026.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care.
citations.narrative:
On February 26, 2026, two juvenile pigs underwent a surgical procedure during which they were placed under anesthesia
and administered isoflurane from a vaporizer with an expired service date of October 2024. The use of equipment that
has not been properly maintained may reduce effectiveness or lead to unintended outcomes. Animals may experience
discomfort or distress resulting in unexpected side effects associated with anesthesia equipment that has not been
adequately serviced. Each research facility shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include
the availability of appropriate facilities, personnel, equipment and services to comply with the provisions of this
subchapter. To be corrected from this date forward on 2/27/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(7) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The attending veterinarian notified the inspector that an IACUC approved protocol had not been followed by a principle 
investigator. The protocol covers training by the Animal Resources Program staff with various species housed at UAB. On 
February 9, 2026, two non-naïve rabbits (rabbits that have undergone procedures) were transferred to this protocol and 
used for a training lab. At the time of training, the protocol indicated that animals used would be naïve rabbits (rabbits that 
would not have undergone procedures prior to use on this protocol). A modification request for the use of non-naïve 
animals was under review but had not been approved by the IACUC at the time of the animal transfer. The IACUC 
determined that no animal welfare concerns occurred at the time of the training lab. The principle investigator has since 
been retrained and the modification to the protocol has now been approved. Failure to follow an approved protocol may 
adversely affect the health and well-being of the animals on the protocol. Implementing changes to protocols without 
IACUC review and approval prevents the IACUC from providing the necessary oversight of animal activities. The IACUC, 
as an agent of the research facility, shall review and approve, require modifications in (to secure approval) or withhold 
approval of proposed significant changes regarding the care and use of animals in ongoing activities. Corrected prior to 
the time of inspection on 2/27/2026. 
2.33(b)(1) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care. 
On February 26, 2026, two juvenile pigs underwent a surgical procedure during which they were placed under anesthesia 
and administered isoflurane from a vaporizer with an expired service date of October 2024. The use of equipment that 
has not been properly maintained may reduce effectiveness or lead to unintended outcomes. Animals may experience 
discomfort or distress resulting in unexpected side effects associated with anesthesia equipment that has not been 
adequately serviced. Each research facility shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include 
the availability of appropriate facilities, personnel, equipment and services to comply with the provisions of this 
subchapter. To be corrected from this date forward on 2/27/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
